record.field: Windmill is an open-source developer platform that turns scripts into APIs, scheduled jobs, multi-step workflows and internal UIs.

Windmill is an open-source developer platform that turns scripts into APIs, scheduled jobs, multi-step workflows and internal UIs.

Developers write small scripts in Python, TypeScript, Go, Bash, SQL and more, and Windmill auto-generates an input form and exposes them as webhooks or background jobs. Scripts can be chained into workflows with branches, retries and approvals, or assembled into full internal apps with a low-code builder. Built on a Rust engine with a PostgreSQL backend, it positions itself as a self-hostable alternative to Retool, Airplane, Pipedream and Temporal.

Key features
  • Polyglot scripting (Python, TypeScript, Go, Bash, SQL, GraphQL, PowerShell, Rust) with auto-generated UIs
  • Flow editor for multi-step workflows with branching, loops, retries, error handling and approval steps
  • Low-code app builder to compose internal tools and dashboards from scripts
  • Triggers via webhooks, cron schedules, message queues and HTTP endpoints
  • Sandboxed execution (nsjail / PID namespace isolation) with per-workspace secret encryption
  • Git sync, RBAC, audit logs and a high-performance Rust worker pool
Best for

Engineering and platform teams that need to build internal tools, automate operations and orchestrate data or DevOps workflows without stitching together separate UI, scheduling and scripting tools.

services: windmill-postgres: image: postgres:16 shm_size: 1g restart: unless-stopped volumes: - ./windmill_postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data environment: POSTGRES_PASSWORD: {{ postgres_password }} POSTGRES_DB: windmill healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - pg_isready -U postgres interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_windmill_postgres' networks: - internal windmill-server: image: ghcr.io/windmill-labs/windmill:main restart: unless-stopped environment: - DATABASE_URL={{ database_url }} - MODE=server - BASE_URL=http://{{ windmill_host }} depends_on: windmill-postgres: condition: service_healthy volumes: - ./windmill_worker_logs:/tmp/windmill/logs container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_windmill_server' networks: - internal windmill-worker: image: ghcr.io/windmill-labs/windmill:main deploy: replicas: 3 resources: limits: cpus: '1' memory: 2048M restart: unless-stopped environment: - DATABASE_URL={{ database_url }} - MODE=worker - WORKER_GROUP=default depends_on: windmill-postgres: condition: service_healthy volumes: - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock - ./windmill_worker_cache:/tmp/windmill/cache - ./windmill_worker_logs:/tmp/windmill/logs container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_windmill_worker' networks: - internal windmill-worker-native: image: ghcr.io/windmill-labs/windmill:main deploy: replicas: 1 resources: limits: cpus: '0.1' memory: 128M restart: unless-stopped environment: - DATABASE_URL={{ database_url }} - MODE=worker - WORKER_GROUP=native - NUM_WORKERS=8 - SLEEP_QUEUE=200 depends_on: windmill-postgres: condition: service_healthy volumes: - ./windmill_worker_logs:/tmp/windmill/logs container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_windmill_worker_native' networks: - internal windmill-lsp: image: ghcr.io/windmill-labs/windmill-lsp:latest restart: unless-stopped volumes: - ./windmill_lsp_cache:/root/.cache container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_windmill_lsp' networks: - internal windmill-caddy: image: ghcr.io/windmill-labs/caddy-l4:latest restart: unless-stopped volumes: - ../files/Caddyfile:/etc/caddy/Caddyfile environment: - BASE_URL=":80" depends_on: - windmill-server - windmill-lsp container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:80' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T windmill-postgres sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d windmill-postgres for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T windmill-postgres sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T windmill-postgres sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T windmill-postgres sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
